meaning: Prostitute. However, it is the most ubiquitous, universal swear word in the Czech language, acting as a comma, an exclamation of anger, or an intensifier.
cultural_context: Used exactly like the Polish 'kurwa'. If a Czech stubbed their toe, this is what they would yell. Highly versatile.
example_usage: To je kurva dobrý! (That is fucking good!)

meaning: Directing something or someone into an anus.
cultural_context: The closest equivalent to shouting 'Dammit!' or 'Fuck!'. Universally used when things go wrong.
example_usage: Do prdele, zapomněl jsem klíče. (Fuck, I forgot the keys.)

meaning: The buttocks or anus (vulgar/slang).
cultural_context: Used constantly in idioms. 'Být v prdeli' (To be in the ass) means to be completely screwed or exhausted. 'Dělat si prdel' (To make an ass) means to joke.
example_usage: Děláš si prdel? (Are you shitting me / joking?)

meaning: Feces. Also meaning 'absolutely nothing'.
cultural_context: Milder swear word. Often used to dismiss someone or state that there is nothing there ('vím hovno' = I know shit/nothing).
example_usage: To je úplný hovno. (That's total bullshit/nothing.)

meaning: Vulgar term for female genitalia.
cultural_context: One of the most vulgar and taboo words in the language. Used to violently insult a woman or describe a horrific situation.
example_usage: Zavři hubu, ty píčo. (Shut your mouth, you cunt.)

meaning: Another extremely vulgar word for female genitalia.
cultural_context: Functionally identical to 'píča', though sometimes used by crude men to describe a highly attractive woman (objectification).
example_usage: To je ale kunda! (What a cunt! [as insult or catcall])

meaning: Penis. Originally meaning rooster.
cultural_context: A very harsh insult against a man. Highly popular in neighboring Slovakia but used identically in Czech.
example_usage: Nebuď takovej kokot. (Don't be such a dickhead.)

meaning: Penis.
cultural_context: The standard Czech equivalent to calling someone a 'dick' or a 'prick'. Deeply insulting.
example_usage: Ten kretén je fakt čurák. (That cretin is a real dick.)

meaning: Derived from 'mrdat' (to fuck), meaning someone who is 'fucked up' or a terrible, deceitful person.
cultural_context: A vicious insult denoting someone with absolutely zero morals. Highly aggressive.
example_usage: Ty zkurvenej zmrde! (You fucking motherfucker!)

meaning: Semen. Also used to describe a terrible situation, object, or person.
cultural_context: A disgusting insult. If a car is completely broken down, it is a 'mrdka'.
example_usage: Moje auto je úplná mrdka. (My car is a complete piece of shit.)

meaning: The toilet. Borrowed from German 'Häusl' (outhouse).
cultural_context: Used to call someone a scoundrel/bastard, or to describe a terrible place.
example_usage: On je pěkný hajzl. (He is a pretty big bastard.)

meaning: Commanding someone to crawl into an anus.
cultural_context: The universal Czech command for someone to step away and leave you alone.
example_usage: Dej mi pokoj, jdi do prdele. (Give me peace, fuck off.)

meaning: Diarrhea or liquid shit. Metaphorically means bullshit, poor quality, or a mess.
cultural_context: Stronger and more visceral than 'hovno'.
example_usage: Tohle jídlo je sračka. (This food is crap.)

meaning: The son of a prostitute.
cultural_context: A direct and venomous personal attack involving someone's mother.
example_usage: Pojď sem, ty zkurvysyne. (Come here, you son of a bitch.)

meaning: The highly vulgar verb for sexual intercourse.
cultural_context: Like 'fuck', it can mean sex, but also not caring about something ('mrdám na to' = I'm fucking it / I don't give a fuck).
example_usage: Mrdám na tuhle práci. (I don't give a fuck about this job.)

meaning: Another Slavic vulgar root for sexual intercourse.
cultural_context: Interchangeable with mrdat. 'Jebni ho!' means 'Hit him/fuck him up!'.
example_usage: Jebu na to. (I fuck on it / I don't give a shit.)

meaning: The adjective form of kurva. Means something that has been ruined or is terrible.
cultural_context: The closest grammatical equivalent to the English adjective 'fucking'.
example_usage: Zkurvenej život. (Fucking life.)

meaning: A noun derived from píča. Means complete nonsense, a total mistake, or a useless trinket.
cultural_context: A brilliantly descriptive word. If you buy a useless plastic gadget, it's a 'píčovina'.
example_usage: To je neuvěřitelná píčovina. (That is incredible bullshit.)

meaning: Same structural concept as píčovina, but derived from kokot (dick).
cultural_context: Nonsense or a stupid idea.
example_usage: Neříkej takový kokotiny. (Don't say such bullshit.)

meaning: To scam, deceive, or cheat someone.
cultural_context: If a mechanic overcharges you, he 'ojebal' you.
example_usage: Chtěli mě ojebat o peníze. (They wanted to fuck me out of my money.)

meaning: To aggressively eliminate, defeat, or deceive someone.
cultural_context: A stronger version of ojebat.
example_usage: Úplně se mnou vyjebal. (He completely fucked me over.)

meaning: To cover with urine.
cultural_context: Used for drunk behavior or severe disrespect.
example_usage: Pochcal se v kalhotách. (He pissed himself in his pants.)

meaning: To urinate oneself. Often used metaphorically for uncontrollable laughter.
cultural_context: Commonly used when recounting a funny story.
example_usage: Budu se asi smíchy pochcat. (I'm going to piss myself laughing.)

meaning: To literal shit on, or metaphorically ruin a situation.
cultural_context: If you fail an exam, you 'posral' it.
example_usage: Všechno jsem posral. (I shit on / fucked up everything.)

meaning: To anger someone deeply.
cultural_context: 'Jsem nasraný' (I am shitted-into) means 'I am extremely angry'.
example_usage: Neser mě. (Don't shit into me / Don't piss me off.)

meaning: Derived from the holy sacrament. A mild curse.
cultural_context: The closest equivalent to 'damn'. Used on TV and around children.
example_usage: Sakra, prší. (Damn, it's raining.)

meaning: An older, almost comedic religious oath referencing the cross.
cultural_context: Mild swearing, often used by frustrated grandfathers.
example_usage: Krucinál fago! (Dammit all!)

meaning: An idiot.
cultural_context: A standard, very common insult.
example_usage: Jsi úplný debil. (You're a complete idiot.)

meaning: A cretin. Slightly sharper than debil.
cultural_context: Derived from the French 'crétin'.
example_usage: Odprejskni, kreténe. (Scram, cretin.)

meaning: A homosexual man.
cultural_context: A derogatory homophobic slur.
example_usage: Jeden starej buzerant. (One old faggot.)

meaning: Originally meaning a servant girl, now exclusively means a slut or prostitute.
cultural_context: Extremely derogatory towards women.
example_usage: Ty zkurvená děvko! (You fucking whore!)

meaning: A female dog.
cultural_context: Used identically to the English word 'bitch'.
example_usage: Arogantní čuba. (Arrogant bitch.)

meaning: The diminutive form of čuba. Actually sounds more venomous and degrading.
cultural_context: Highly common insult.
example_usage: Vypadni, ty čubko. (Get out, you bitch.)

meaning: Plural/genitive form of hovno.
cultural_context: Used in phrases like 'plno hoven' (full of shit).
example_usage: Mluvíš plno hoven. (You're speaking full of shit.)

meaning: Flatulence.
cultural_context: Also used to mean 'nothing' in a child-friendly way. 'Dostaneš prd' = You will get a fart / nothing.
example_usage: To je na prd. (That is on a fart / That is useless.)

meaning: Nonsense, stemming from 'kráva' (cow).
cultural_context: The family-friendly version of píčovina/kokotina. Used openly on TV.
example_usage: Nedělej kraviny. (Don't do cow-things / nonsense.)

meaning: A cow. Used to insult a stubborn or stupid woman.
cultural_context: A classic, slightly dated male insult toward annoying women.
example_usage: Ta hloupá kráva. (That stupid cow.)

meaning: A man who chases prostitutes or cheats on his partner constantly.
cultural_context: A strong condemnation of a man's fidelity.
example_usage: Všichni vědí, že je to kurevník. (Everyone knows he's a whoremonger.)

meaning: The vocative case of 'zmrd'. Used when yelling directly at someone.
cultural_context: Czech grammar requires the vocative case for addressing people. Shouting 'Zmrde!' is highly aggressive.
example_usage: Zhebnij, zmrde! (Drop dead, motherfucker!)

meaning: A slightly softer, older slang term for having sex.
cultural_context: Sounds funny rather than intensely vulgar.
example_usage: Celou noc šoustali. (They shagged all night.)

meaning: Originally meant 'to tidy up / look in corners' in older Czech (and still does in standard Polish!), but in modern Czech it exclusively means to fuck.
cultural_context: A deadly false friend with Polish, causing immense confusion between the two nations.
example_usage: Jdeme šukat. (We're going to fuck.)

meaning: Female breasts. Slang derived from goats.
cultural_context: Highly common anatomical slang.
example_usage: Má velký kozy. (She has big goats/tits.)

meaning: Breasts. Derives from animal teats.
cultural_context: Similar to kozy, crude male slang.
example_usage: Ukaž cecky. (Show your jugs.)

meaning: Command to leave immediately.
cultural_context: Not inherently vulgar, but highly hostile. Often paired with 'do prdele'.
example_usage: Vypadni odtud! (Get the hell out of here!)

meaning: A famine. Metaphorically a place that is miserable or empty.
cultural_context: Dark humor usage.
example_usage: Tady je samej hladomor. (It's just hunger-death in here.)

meaning: A direct structural translation of 'kiss my ass'.
cultural_context: Universal defiance.
example_usage: Víš co? Polib mi prdel. (You know what? Kiss my ass.)

meaning: A creative expansion of the central curse. Something completely worthless or absurd.
cultural_context: Shows off the Slavic penchant for poetic obscenity.
example_usage: To stojí za kundu z ryby. (That is worth a fish's cunt.)

meaning: A softer slang word for buzerant.
cultural_context: Often used among straight men to mock someone for acting weak or effeminate.
example_usage: Nebreč, ty buzno. (Don't cry, you sissy.)

meaning: Something completely crazy or chaotic. Derived from 'magor' (lunatic).
cultural_context: When a situation is impossibly stressful or stupid.
example_usage: Tohle je čistá magořina. (This is pure insanity.)

meaning: Someone who is crazy or acts dangerously unhinged.
cultural_context: Common insult in traffic.
example_usage: Ten chlap je úplnej magor. (That guy is a total psycho.)

meaning: Someone who brags too much.
cultural_context: Very gentle, polite disapproval.
example_usage: Je to jen hloupý chvástal. (He's just a stupid braggart.)

meaning: To completely break or ruin a situation or object.
cultural_context: Derived from kurva. Means something is 'whored up' / ruined.
example_usage: Úplně jsi to zkurvil. (You completely fucked it up.)

meaning: A socially inept or foolish person.
cultural_context: A classic, TV-friendly insult.
example_usage: Takový blbec. (Such an idiot.)

meaning: A portmanteau of mam (my/mother) and mrd (fuck).
cultural_context: A creative, slightly less common alternative to zmrd.
example_usage: Drž hubu, ty mamrde. (Shut your mouth, you motherfucker.)

meaning: A naive or slightly stupid girl.
cultural_context: Almost affectionate, mostly condescending.
example_usage: Je to taková hloupá nanynka. (She's such a silly little bimbo.)

meaning: A vain, superficial, and unintelligent young woman.
cultural_context: More insulting than nanynka. Usually implies heavy makeup and low IQ.
example_usage: Ta namyšlená pipina. (That conceited bimbo.)

english_equivalent: fucked up / stoned out of one's mind
meaning: Someone who looks exhausted or heavily under the influence of drugs.
cultural_context: Youth slang.
example_usage: Pepa byl úplně vyještěnej. (Pepa was completely fucked up/stoned.)

meaning: Tripe soup is popular in Czechia. Slang for someone's mouth or face.
cultural_context: 'Dát někomu přes dršťku' means 'To punch someone in the face/tripe'.
example_usage: Drž tu svou dršťku. (Shut your ugly mug.)

meaning: An animal's mouth. Used to insult a person's mouth.
cultural_context: Vocative case. Often used to tell someone to shut up.
example_usage: Zavři tlamu! (Shut your trap/maw!)

meaning: A historic wall in Prague built by Charles IV to employ the starving poor. Relates to pointless work.
cultural_context: Not vulgar, deeply Czech idiom.
example_usage: Tohle je jak stavět hladovou zeď. (This is like building the hunger wall.)

meaning: A more graphic version of kiss my ass.
cultural_context: Extremely hostile and repulsive.
example_usage: Vyliž mi prdel, hajzle. (Lick my ass, you bastard.)

meaning: Semen/sperm.
cultural_context: Medical term used vulgarly.
example_usage: Smrdí to jak sperma. (It smells like cum.)

meaning: A diminutive, cutesy version of shit. Used to mock an insignificant man.
cultural_context: Condescending and humiliating rather than actively brutal.
example_usage: Takový hovňousek mi nebude poroučet. (A little turd like that won't give me orders.)

meaning: From German 'Gesicht' (face). In Czech, it means an ugly or stupid face.
cultural_context: Widely used slang.
example_usage: Dám ti pěstí do ksichtu. (I'll punch you in the mug.)

meaning: A literal translation of the English / Latin 'son of a bitch'.
cultural_context: Milder than zkurvysyn, often used in dubbed American movies.
example_usage: Kde je ten čubčí syn? (Where is that son of a bitch?)

meaning: A highly creative old-school compound word for someone who sucks penises.
cultural_context: 'Hulit' means to smoke/suck, 'brk' means a quill/joint. Metaphorically incredible.
example_usage: Ty jeden hulibrku. (You cocksucker.)

meaning: Something terribly painful or bad.
cultural_context: A 'clean' extreme adjective.
example_usage: To je ukrutná bolest. (That is atrocious pain.)

meaning: The ultimate state of failure or exhaustion.
cultural_context: If your phone breaks, it is 'v prdeli'. If you lose your job, you are 'v prdeli'.
example_usage: Jsme úplně v prdeli. (We are completely fucked.)

meaning: To abandon a project or stop caring about a situation.
cultural_context: Child-friendly version of 'mrdat na to'.
example_usage: Prdím na to, jdu spát. (I'm farting on it / screw it, I'm going to sleep.)

meaning: A cat. Often used to refer to a sexually attractive woman.
cultural_context: A catcall rather than a curse, but highly demeaning depending on context.
example_usage: Koukej na tu kočičku. (Look at that hot chick.)

meaning: The male version of a cunt. A despicable man.
cultural_context: One of the most offensive things you can call a male in Czech.
example_usage: Ten šéf je zasraný píčus. (That boss is a fucking dickhead/cunt.)

meaning: A loud, cheap, uneducated woman.
cultural_context: Very specific classist insult against a woman acting without any grace.
example_usage: Řve jako píča z tržnice. (She's yelling like a marketplace whore.)

meaning: To violently destroy something or beat someone up.
cultural_context: Adding 'roz-' (apart/to pieces) to the fuck root.
example_usage: Rozjebu ti ksicht! (I'll smash your fucking face in!)

meaning: Russian/Slavic universal curse for female genitalia.
cultural_context: Fully understood in Czech, used interchangeably with píča but sounds slightly more foreign/Eastern.
example_usage: Zasraná pizda. (Fucking cunt.)

meaning: Something covered in feces.
cultural_context: Functions as a strong adjective of dislike. 'Zasraný zloděj' (Fucking/shitty thief).
example_usage: Tenhle zasraný počítač nefunguje. (This shitty computer isn't working.)

english_equivalent: to fuck up / to stow away roughly
meaning: To hit someone or to shove an object violently.
cultural_context: Another prefix form of jebat.
example_usage: Já mu jednu zajebu. (I'll fuck him one / punch him one.)

meaning: A tough guy or hired muscle.
cultural_context: Crime slang.
example_usage: Hraje si na bouchače. (He plays at being a thug.)

meaning: To have sex with someone.
cultural_context: Colloquial and direct.
example_usage: Tě ošukám. (I'll bone you.)
